titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Gabriel Paris Collection Relating to France, May 1968
Paris (Gabriel) Collection Relating to France, May 1968
titlestmt.author: by Mark Custer

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This finding aid, which is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0., has a MARC record in Yale's ILS with the following bib number: 11345517.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Tuesday April 20, 2021 at 03:10

did.unittitle: Gabriel Paris collection relating to France, May 1968

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

bioghist.head: Gabriel Paris (1924-2000)
bioghist.p: Gabriel Paris was an artist, painter, and activist. He was born in Uzès, France, but spent most of his life in Paris, where he died in 2000.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Gabriel Paris Collection Relating to France, May 1968 is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into four series: I. Sketchbooks, 1968-1974. II. Photographs, 1968. III. Political tracts, pamphlets, and newspaper clippings, 1968-1978. IV. Posters, lithographs, and maquettes, 1968-1970.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Gabriel Paris collection consists of materials related to the May 1968 protests in Paris, France. The collection contains three screen print posters produced at the Atelier populaire (ex-École des Beaux-Arts) in May-June 1968, several signed lithographs produced between May-July 1968, eleven original maquettes (1968-1970), and two sketchbooks created by Gabriel Paris, each of which contains numerous drawings and sketches that were used in the production of protest posters. The collection also contains 45 black and white photographic prints taken by Paris, which document the May 1968 protests, and over 200 printed political tracts, which were collected by Paris from 1968-1978.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.

This collection received a basic level of processing, including in some instances rehousing and minimal organization, when it arrived at the library. Information included in the Scope and Contents note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Gabriel Paris Collection Relating to France, May 1968. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Glenn Horowitz Bookseller, Inc., 2012.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Each sketchbook contains a variety of poster art sketches and designs, many of which are cut out and affixed directly to the pages.

did.unittitle: Chronique a fleur de peau Mai 68–June 69

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Title is printed on the bottom of the cover, in black ink. Also printed on the cover, in pencil: "Gabriel Paris: Chronique a fleur de peau 1968–74 / Notes et croquis."
Note from the dealer: Chronique a fleur de peau was the proposed title for an autobiographical film that Paris had been working on since the mid-seventies until the time of his death in 2000.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Title printed on bottom of cover, in black ink. The sketchbook also contains a few newspaper clippings, including an editorial piece by Robert Merle from Le Monde , dated 1974 May 25.

did.physdesc: 45 photographic prints, black and white, 7.5 x 8 cm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The photographs are affixed to four separate cardboard pages.

did.unittitle: Political tracts, pamphlets, and newspaper clippings

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a variety of items collected by Gabriel Paris from 1968-1978. Over twenty of the items were published by a community of artists, "MontmARTre aux artistes," of which Gabriel Paris was a member. A few pamphlets detail the 1970s arrest of 3 labor activists -- Houdet, Rousseau, and Ordennez-Aurioles -- who were arrested for putting up posters demanding the release of Devaux, Herve, Trouilleux and Divet. The collection also includes publications attributed to the Jeunesse Communiste Revolutionnaire (JCR), Parti Socialiste Unifie (PSU), Parti Communiste Francais (PCF), L'Union Naitional des Etudiants de France (UNEF), and more.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes three clippings and a copy of l'Humanité (two-page special edition), dated Monday, 1977 September 26.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Titles were assigned based on textual phrases that are present in each artwork. If a poster does not contain any text, it was left untitled, with descriptive keywords added in square brackets. Many of the posters, however, contain multiple phrases. In those cases, preference was given to any phrases that appeared on other posters in the collection. For example, the three posters titled "Je regarde l'avenir eclater dans les yeux" are similar compositions, but not identical; each contains a different set of phrases, as well as a slight variation in imagery.

did.physdesc: Lithograph. On cardstock. Red and black ink.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Signed "47/50 Gabriel Paris" in pencil. Includes a long quote attributed to "Combat no. 7423, Michel Ragon ," as well as the following text: '20 " 07 Gare de Lyon - Vendredi, 24 Mai 1968.'

did.physdesc: Original maquette. Black ink. Poster board.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Stamped "© Gabriel Paris, tout droits réservés."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Signed "Gabriel Paris." Stamped "Chronique a fleur de peau. Gabriel Paris."

did.physdesc: Original maquette. Black ink and masking tape.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Signed "GP"

did.unittitle: Les CRS VEAUtent VOUi à De Gaulle
did.physdesc: Screen print poster. Black ink. Newsprint.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Stamped "Atelier C8-H40."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Stamped "27 Avril 1969." Also includes repetitions of the word "NON" at the top of the poster.

did.physdesc: Lithograph. Blue, red, and black ink.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Signed "32/40 Gabriel Paris" in pencil.

did.unittitle: De Gaulle n'est plus seul (recto); Untitled collage (verso)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Depicts Richard Nixon's head, in cartoonish style, with Viet-Nam written on his forehead; on both sides of his head, there are a number of other geopolitical references, including Formosa, Greece, and Spain.

did.unittitle: Il ne reste plus qu'à peindre des fleurs
did.physdesc: Lithograph. Black, yellow, red, blue, and green ink.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Signed "45/50 Gabriel Paris" in pencil.

did.physdesc: Original maquette. Black and white ink (recto); Black, yellow, and red ink (verso).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Recto: includes a drawing of a parrot, which is being broadcast on television, delivering the following line: "Le Gaullisme rien que le Gaullisme et toujours le Gaullisme." Verso: stamped "Gabriel Paris, Mai / Juin 1968".

did.unittitle: Je regarde l'avenir eclater dans les yeux
did.physdesc: Original maquette. Black, red, and white ink.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Dated "1.6.68." Dimensions written in pencil, top left. Also has the following: "Poems de Mai 7.6.1968"

did.unittitle: Je regarde l'avenir eclater dans les yeux

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes other phrases, such as "Le Chienlit c'est lui," and "Le Pouvoir est dans la rue."

did.unittitle: Je regarde l'avenir eclater dans les yeux

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Stamped "Gabriel Paris Mai 1968 / Ex-ecole des beaux arts."

did.unittitle: Leur Campagne Commence / Notre Lutte Continue

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Stamped "Atelier Populaire Ex-Ecole Des Beaux-Arts."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Stamped "Atelier C8-H40."

did.unittitle: Toi mon camarade toi que j'ignorais, viens parle-nous / Contestation
did.physdesc: Original maquette. Black, red, and gray ink. On heavy cardboard. Folded in half.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Signed "Gabriel Paris Mai 1968."

did.unittitle: Toi mon camarade toi que j'ignorais, viens parle-nous / Contestation
did.physdesc: Black and white reproduction poster on cardstock.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Signed "Gabriel Paris Mai 1968". Dimensions written in pencil.

did.unittitle: Toi mon camarade toi que j'ignorais, viens parle-nous
did.physdesc: Original maquette. Red and black ink.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Signed "38/45 Gabriel Paris". Includes the phrase, "Dix ans c'est asser / dix ans c'est trop," as well as others.

did.physdesc: Red, black and gray ink. Folded in half.
On canvas. Black, yellow, red, and gray ink.
On cardboard. Black, yellow, red and gray ink.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Signed "31/40 Gabriel Paris" in pencil.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Collage of five newspaper clippings, primarily photographic. The only clipping with text has the following printed on it, "Paris Match No. 314 Du 2 Au 9 Avril 1955." On the top right of the poster, "compo 52 x 72" is written in pencil; "LAPIN" is written in the center.

did.physdesc: Black, gray and red ink. Cardboard cutout.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "compos 52 x 72" written in pencil, top right.

did.physdesc: Red and black ink, with newspaper print.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "compo 52 x 72" written in pencil
